CREATE TABLE test_table (A INT, B FLOAT);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 10.0), (2, 5.0), (3, 8.0);
SELECT argMin(A, B) FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE nested_table (C INT, D FLOAT);
INSERT INTO nested_table VALUES (1, 15.0), (1, 12.0), (2, 9.0);
SELECT argMin(C, D) FROM (SELECT C, D FROM nested_table WHERE D < 15.0);
CREATE TABLE another_table (E INT, F FLOAT);
INSERT INTO another_table VALUES (1, 20.0), (1, 18.0), (2, 6.0);
SELECT argMin(A, B) FROM test_table JOIN another_table ON test_table.A = another_table.E;
CREATE TABLE more_data (G INT, H FLOAT);
INSERT INTO more_data VALUES (1, 7.0), (2, 14.0), (3, 2.0);
SELECT argMin(A, B) FROM test_table, more_data WHERE test_table.A = more_data.G;
CREATE TABLE complex_table (I INT, J FLOAT);
INSERT INTO complex_table VALUES (1, 30.5), (1, 25.0), (2, 40.0);
SELECT argMin(I, J) FROM (SELECT I, J FROM complex_table WHERE I = 1 ORDER BY J DESC) AS ordered;
CREATE TABLE multi_join_table1 (K INT, L FLOAT);
CREATE TABLE multi_join_table2 (M INT, N FLOAT);
INSERT INTO multi_join_table1 VALUES (1, 50.0), (2, 30.0);
INSERT INTO multi_join_table2 VALUES (1, 20.0), (2, 10.0);
SELECT argMin(K, L) FROM multi_join_table1 JOIN multi_join_table2 ON multi_join_table1.K = multi_join_table2.M WHERE L > 25.0;
